title: Update working memory from Chrome changelog UX session
summary: Durable context from a session that added Chrome Web Store rating UX and image lightbox previews to the changelog modal.

claims.text: The version changelog popup is owned by the content changelog module: `showChangelogModal` loads the matching markdown note, extracts the current language section, sanitizes the rendered HTML with DOMPurify, then passes the sanitized HTML and language into `createChangelogModal`.
The changelog popup's Chrome Web Store rating prompt is intended to appear only for Chrome users, and its prompt and CTA copy should be selected from the user's current language.

`createChangelogModal` appends a Chrome Web Store rating banner between the recommendation text and footer action row only when `isChrome()` is true; the banner links to the Gemini Voyager Chrome Web Store listing and uses `changelog_rate_chrome` plus `changelog_rate_chrome_cta` translations for the active language.

`isChrome()` is a browser utility for Chrome Web Store prompts: it first excludes Safari via `isSafari()`, then treats `chrome` or `chromium` user agents as Chrome while excluding Edge and Firefox user agents.
Images inside the changelog popup are intended to show a pointer affordance on hover and open an enlarged preview when clicked.

After inserting sanitized changelog HTML, `createChangelogModal` binds every body image to `showImageLightbox`; the lightbox appends a full-screen overlay to `document.body`, renders the clicked image, closes on overlay click or Escape, and removes its keydown listener during close.

Injected changelog CSS gives body images a `zoom-in` cursor and hover opacity feedback, and styles `.gv-changelog-lightbox` as a fixed full-screen dark overlay with centered constrained image preview and `zoom-out` cursor.

sources.title: Claude Code session 96b73801 Chrome store popup and extension UX updates

metadata.reason: The user asked for a graceful Chrome-only prompt in the updated version popup that invites Chrome users to rate the extension in the Google store, with language-specific text based on the selected language.
The user explicitly requested click-to-enlarge preview behavior for images in the popup and a changed cursor when hovering over images.
